topics.title: Signals Won 2026 — Except in React: A Fine-Grained Reactivity Guide
topics.whyTrending: TC39 Signals proposal + Svelte 5 runes, SolidJS, Angular 20, Vue 4 all converging on fine-grained reactivity while React bets on the compiler. Controversial and uncovered.

topics.title: View Transitions API: Native Page Animations Without a Framework
topics.whyTrending: Cross-document View Transitions now baseline in browsers; teams ripping out Framer Motion for native transitions.

topics.title: INP: The Core Web Vital That Replaced FID (And Why Your App Fails It)
topics.whyTrending: Interaction to Next Paint is the metric Google now ranks on; most React apps still measure the wrong thing.

topics.title: Local-First Web Apps in 2026: Sync Engines, CRDTs, and No More Spinners
topics.whyTrending: Zero, ElectricSQL, and Yjs made local-first practical; the biggest architectural shift after RSC.

topics.title: The Rust Toolchain Takeover: Rolldown, Oxc and Vite's Rewrite
topics.whyTrending: Vite migrating to Rolldown, Oxc replacing Babel/ESLint; VoidZero consolidating the JS toolchain in Rust.

topics.title: Modern CSS in 2026: :has(), @scope and Container Queries Killed Your JS
topics.whyTrending: Parent selectors, scoped styles, and container queries now baseline — removing whole categories of JS hacks.

topics.title: Vue 4 Vapor Mode: The Virtual DOM Is Optional Now
topics.whyTrending: Vue 4 ships Vapor mode (no vdom, signals-driven), closing the gap to Solid/Svelte on benchmarks.

topics.title: Astro Islands vs React Server Components: Two Bets on Less JavaScript
topics.whyTrending: Astro 5 surging for content sites; direct architectural contrast with the RSC model.

topics.title: HTMX and the Hypermedia Revival: Is the SPA Era Ending?
topics.whyTrending: Ongoing HN debate on hypermedia-driven apps vs heavy SPAs; strong opinion/controversy angle.

topics.title: Biome vs ESLint + Prettier: The Rust Toolchain Migration Guide
topics.whyTrending: Biome 2.x with type-aware linting is a real ESLint+Prettier replacement; measurable CI speedups.
